title: Field Briefing: Raster Purpose and Vector Meaning
summary: Field Briefing: Raster Purpose and Vector Meaning uses an original image and vector evidence briefing scenario to retrieve earlier HTML skills, add a bounded artifact layer, and verify transfer with source and browser evidence.
objectives: Retrieve html images alt, html svg without step-by-step imitation.
Produce and verify a real image and vector evidence briefing artifact or evidence report.
Preserve earlier valid requirements while diagnosing changed content and constraints.

options.text: Image and vector evidence briefing case 1: omitting alt is the recommended way to mark an image decorative.

image and vector evidence briefing phase 2.4: test the surrounding content with the image unavailable

steps.title: Read the image and vector evidence briefing contract · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 4: Which practice follows the explanation and remains valid in a changed document? Work context: image and vector evidence briefing.
steps.why: Field Briefing: Raster Purpose and Vector Meaning requires this decision because each decision must preserve document meaning, user control, portability, and inspectable evidence. This is checkpoint 4 in the image and vector evidence briefing.

content.text: image and vector evidence briefing: Alternative text communicates an image’s purpose in context. Informative images need a concise equivalent; functional images name the action; complex images need a nearby extended explanation; and decorative images use an empty alt. Intrinsic width and height reserve the correct aspect ratio before the image loads. SVG can be loaded as an external image or embedded inline. Inline SVG exposes its internal structure to the document and can receive an accessible name; decorative SVG should be hidden from assistive technology. A viewBox defines scalable coordinates independently of rendered size.
